lesson: A dead machine in a hot, enclosed corner with no continuity past the compressor’s thermal cutout just needs the cutout reset — but fix the airflow or it trips again. It is not a compressor fault.
symptoms: Machine completely dead — no display, no response.
Machine is in a warm, enclosed corner of the kitchen.

tests.error-log: Can't pull the log — the panel is dark.
tests.temp-probe: Rear of the machine hot to the touch; compressor body very warm.
tests.continuity-test: Supply live into the machine, but no continuity through the compressor circuit past the thermal cutout.

flavour: Machine was fine. Machine was hot. Machine is fine again.
authenticityNote: Thermal cutouts on compressor motors trip when ventilation is blocked by the machine's location. Resetting without improving airflow means it trips again — find and fix the root cause before leaving.
